query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer:
I've completed the research and submitted my findings.

**Bottom line:** No new cases have been reported in Uganda after 21 June 2026, and the Uganda total has **not** risen above 20.

- **Uganda: 20 confirmed cases, 2 deaths** — unchanged since 21 June 2026.
- The last confirmed Uganda case was identified on **21 June 2026**, with no new cases since (confirmed by both ECDC and WHO).
- **WHO DON612** (published 3 July 2026, data as of 2 July 2026) confirms 20 confirmed cases + 2 deaths + 1 probable death; 821 contacts had completed 21-day follow-up.
- **ECDC** (outbreak page updated 6 July 2026, data as of 5 July 2026) confirms the same 20-case figure and explicitly states "no new cases have been reported since" 21 June. **CDTR Week 27** (27 June–3 July 2026) was published with a consistent Ebola update.
- Uganda's outbreak is stabilizing (16 recovered; most contacts cleared follow-up), while DRC continues rising (1,561 confirmed cases, 506 deaths as of 4 July 2026).

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 5 July 2026... a total of 20 confirmed cases, including two deaths, have been reported by the Ministry of Health in Uganda. The last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda (DON612)
sources.snippet: As of 2 July 2026, a cumulative of 20 confirmed cases including two deaths in imported cases and one probable case who has died. The last confirmed case was identified on 21 June 2026. 821 contacts have completed their 21-day follow-up.

sources.title: Communicable disease threats report, 27 June - 3 July 2026, Week 27
sources.snippet: ECDC CDTR Week 27 covering 27 June - 3 July 2026, includes updates on Ebola among other threats.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic
sources.snippet: PHEIC declared by WHO on 16 May 2026. 506 confirmed deaths in DRC and two deaths in Uganda as of the first week of July. Caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus.

sources.title: Ebola Map — Live Global Ebola Outbreak Tracker
sources.snippet: As of 26 June: Uganda 20 confirmed cases and 2 deaths; DRC 1,155 confirmed cases and 304 confirmed deaths (per ECDC).

sources.title: Bundibugyo virus disease updates - NICD
sources.snippet: As of 4 July 2026, health authorities reported 1561 laboratory-confirmed cases (including 506 deaths) in DRC provinces of Ituri, North Kivu and South Kivu.

data_points.metric: Combined outbreak confirmed cases (DRC + Uganda + France)
